data.description:
## Context / Problem
A PASS from `rebar review-plan` is supposed to end with a signed attestation (the guide: 'on a non-blocking PASS, signs an attestation'). In practice, under concurrent store activity, the review completes PASS but exits unsigned. Observed 5 times in one session (2026-07-23, session log 1b86-3286-bbf9-4a8f) while re-planning epic bbf1 with parallel reviews running: tickets 97f2-2d98-2eff-41c1, eefd-49b1-995f-488d, aff0-b0d9-e11c-4b6a, 625b-9cd1-1ce9-4e5f, and epic bbf1-82e1-cf9d-494a all printed 'plan review PASSED but the attestation could not be persisted: plan review generation changed; re-review required' (src/rebar/_cli/_llm_commands.py:121) or logged 'attestation signing failed; verdict unsigned' (src/rebar/llm/plan_review/__init__.py:738). Only reviews with zero concurrent store movement (e.g. ad44-9f46-07aa-480c's re-run) self-signed.

## Mechanism (read from the tree)
src/rebar/llm/plan_review/generation.py:146-154: at sign time the plan-relation generation snapshot `collect(ticket_id)` is compared against `initial_generation` captured at review START; if ANY tracked material moved during the (minutes-long) LLM review, it raises PlanReviewGenerationChanged('plan review generation changed; re-review required') and the verdict stays unsigned. But the sanctioned recovery, `rebar sign-review <id>`, then signs from the SAME REVIEW_RESULT sidecar against the CURRENT head with no LLM re-run — and the claim gate accepts it ('re-signed the plan-review attestation from the latest REVIEW_RESULT sidecar'). So the in-command fail-closed check is strictly harsher than the invariant the store actually enforces, and the message's 're-review required' is contradicted by the recovery path that needs no re-review. Under multi-agent load (many parallel review-plan/edit/comment auto-commits) the majority of passing reviews hit this and each needs a manual rescue.

## Expected
Either (a) review-plan self-heals: on PlanReviewGenerationChanged, if the ticket's own material fingerprint is unchanged (only unrelated store movement), retry the sign against the current head — i.e. run the sign-review path in-process before giving up; or (b) if a genuine material change to the ticket occurred, keep failing closed but say so specifically; and in the residual failure message, instruct 'rebar sign-review <id>' (the documented recovery) instead of 're-review required' (wrong — a re-review is not required and wastes a full LLM run).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A review-plan PASS during which only UNRELATED store writes land (another ticket's edits/comments/attestations) ends certified with no manual step — verified by a unit/integration test that commits an unrelated ticket event between review start and sign.
- [ ] A review-plan PASS during which the reviewed ticket's own material changes still fails closed, with a message naming the ticket-material change — verified by a companion test.
- [ ] The residual unsigned-PASS message points to 'rebar sign-review <id>', not 're-review required' — verified by asserting the CLI output text (src/rebar/_cli/_llm_commands.py:121).
- [ ] make lint && make typecheck && make test green.
data.title: review-plan: passing review frequently ends unsigned under concurrent store writes; error demands re-review although sign-review certifies from the same sidecar
